title: hieracium spathulatum subsp. ilergabonum
intro: Hieracium spathulatum subsp. ilergabonum, also known as Ilergabon Hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium genus, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. Traditionally, Ilergabon Hawkweed has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and reducing swelling. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Ilergabon Hawkweed can be prepared as a herbal infusion or tincture, with the leaves and flowers typically being used for medicinal purposes.
